title: Control-plane hardening: path containment for trusted checked-in requirements_file/verification_file references (LOW-1)
objective: Add explicit path-containment validation for the directive manifest's requirements_file and verification_file references so they cannot resolve outside the directive's own directory, while preserving the current fail-closed behavior. Narrowly scoped control-plane hardening; NOT a blocker for the first wave; not implemented under D-002.
business_reason: D-002 Section 2: preserve the LOW-1 review finding durably in project control; do not amend PR #97 again for it.
outputs: tools/directive_registry.py and/or tools/validate_directive_compliance.py (explicit path-containment check on manifest.requirements_file / verification_file, resolving within the directive's own directory)
forbidden_paths: CLAUDE.md, .claude/**, .github/**, any other task's paths or report
the D-002 registry source files (append-only) and any other directive's records
acceptance_scenarios: AS-1 (executable): a manifest whose requirements_file or verification_file resolves outside its directive directory (e.g. '../other/x.json' or an absolute path) is REJECTED by the validator with a clear containment error.
AS-2 (executable): the current, well-formed in-directory references continue to PASS unchanged (no regression); fail-closed behavior preserved.
